mnemonics: My Very Educated Mother Just Served Us Nine Pizzas (Like Bitcoin's Genesis Block) - Version, Previous Hash, Merkle Root, Timestamp, nBits, Nonce, (and more).
keyPoints: The block header is an 80-byte hash that uniquely identifies a block on the blockchain.
It contains a summary of the entire block's data, most importantly through the Merkle Root.
Miners change the Nonce value repeatedly to try and get the block's hash below the target threshold set by nBits.
commonMistakes: Confusing the Merkle Root (a hash of all transactions) with the Block Hash (a hash of the header).
Thinking the Nonce is the only thing miners can change; they can also alter the coinbase transaction to change the Merkle Root.
